name: Snodd
author: Dr Eric Silverman (2021) / AGP port
description: Snodd, by Dr Eric Silverman (2021): Luis Bolaños Mures' Xodd/Yodd ported to the snub square tiling. Players place one or two stones of EITHER colour on an empty board of snub-square points (Red moves first, and only one stone on the opening turn); each interior point has degree 5, between Xodd's square grid and Yodd's hex. A group is a set of connected like-coloured stones, and at the end of every turn the TOTAL number of groups on the board must be odd. You may pass when it keeps the count odd; two passes in a row end the game, and whoever has FEWER groups of their own colour wins. Because the total is odd, the two counts can never tie — there are no draws. Rendered on the dual Cairo pentagonal tiling.
